channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Plane Crashes on Texas Highway
item.description: People rushed to pull survivors from a private jet that crashed and caught fire on a highway in Laredo, Texas, on Tuesday night, leaving one dead.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

media:content.media:credit: Zayra Garza, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: People trying to pull survivors out of a small plane that crashed on a highway in Laredo, Texas, on Tuesday.

item.title: Argentina Fans Celebrate Messi-Led Victory Over Algeria
item.description: Lionel Messi scored his first World Cup hat trick as Argentina beat Algeria 3-0 in their opening match on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Charlotte Wilson/Getty Images

item.title: Live Updates: G7 Leaders Praise Trump for U.S.-Iran Deal
item.description: Leaders from the Group of 7 nations, who have been courting President Trump at a summit in France, called the preliminary agreement a “breakthrough” secured under the president’s “strong leadership.”
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kristalina Georgieva, the head of the International Monetary Fund, speaks with some leaders of G7 countries in Evian-les-Bains, France, on Wednesday.

item.title: Georgia Republicans Choose Trump’s Pick for Senate, but Not Governor
item.description: Republicans in Georgia advanced Trump’s pick for senator but not governor in the state’s runoff primary on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump’s chosen candidates won three Republican Senate primaries on Tuesday, but he but ran into trouble in other races in Georgia.

item.title: ¿Vale la pena tomar suplementos de glutatión?
item.description: La llamada “madre de todos los antioxidantes” se vende en tiras solubles, cápsulas, líquidos, geles y polvos. Hemos preguntado a los expertos si realmente funcionan.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wright

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 17, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Gilgo Beach Serial Killer Will Receive Life Sentence After Guilty Plea
item.description: The decision on Wednesday will bring to an end an infamous case that took investigators more than a decade to solve.
item.dc:creator: Corey Kilgannon

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by James Carbone
media:content.media:description: Rex Heuermann pleaded guilty to murdering eight women in the so-called Gilgo Beach killings at a court hearing in April.

item.title: The Other Grand Army Plaza Gets a $21 Million Makeover
item.description: An overlooked plaza that opened more than a century ago and was designed as “an outdoor living room” at the southeast corner of Central Park re-emerges after a lengthy restoration.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu

media:content.media:credit: Anna Connors for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The renovation of the Grand Army Plaza in Manhattan was 13 years in the making.

item.title: How Israel Came to Dominate a Congressional Primary Campaign in New York
item.description: Brad Lander is challenging Representative Dan Goldman over his stance on the war in Gaza as the two men face off in a contentious race for a Brooklyn and Lower Manhattan seat.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack

media:content.media:credit: Eduardo Munoz/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Israel Day parade provided an example of the divide over Israel between the candidates in a closely watched House race: Representative Dan Goldman attended, while his opponent, Brad Lander, did not.

item.title: A Tournament on Native Land Makes Millions. A Tribe Wants a Bigger Cut.
item.description: The Shinnecock Hills Golf Club on Long Island earns millions when it hosts the U.S. Open. But the nearby Indian tribe has for years only received a fraction of that.
item.dc:creator: Corey Kilgannon

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lisa Goree, the chairwoman of the Shinnecock Indian Nation, why says the golf course hosting the U.S. Open this week on Long Island was built on their land.

item.title: We Want to Hear from Indigenous People Across New York.
item.description: The New York Times is examining the relationship between New York State officials and Native Americans.
item.dc:creator: Jay Root and Bianca Pallaro

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Harry Wallace, the chief of the Unkechaug Indian Nation, wants state officials to remove artwork from the Capitol depicting Indigenous peoples.

item.title: Why The New York Times Is Expanding in Texas
item.description: Members of a new team of Times reporters and editors are covering culture, economics and more in a state long known for its political clout.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Zachary Balcoff/The New York Times; Sandy Carson for The New York Times, Antranik Tavitian for The New York Times, Desiree Rios for The New York Times, Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times, Mike Osborne for The New York Times

item.title: Ucrania usa la IA para derribar drones de Rusia
item.description: Los interceptores demuestran que Ucrania apuesta por tecnologías autónomas entrenadas con enormes cantidades de datos de guerra.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Preparándose para lanzar una réplica ligera de un dron Shahed de fabricación rusa para ser interceptado.

item.title: Bob Dylan y Liza Minnelli ya cumplieron 80 años, y tienen algo que decirle a Trump
item.description: “Me siento igual que hace 50 años”, dijo el presidente Trump. “Es una locura”.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Opinion

item.title: Word of the Day: sinecure
item.description: This word has appeared in three articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Late Night Wades Into the Reflecting Pool’s Algae Bloom
item.description: President Trump “promised he would drain the swamp,” Jimmy Kimmel said. “Instead, he spent 14 million of our dollars building a new one.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: The World Cup Gives Kansas City a Rare Turn in the Global Spotlight
item.description: The tournament brings unique challenges and upsides for Kansas City, the smallest U.S. metro area to host matches.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:credit: Bernadett Szabo/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Argentina fans celebrate after the match in Kansas City, Mo., on Tuesday.

item.title: 6 conclusiones de la investigación del Times sobre la muerte de Epstein
item.description: Analizamos todas las teorías plausibles sobre su muerte, tanto las oficiales como las alternativas, en busca de los argumentos y las pruebas más convincentes.
item.dc:creator: Charles Homans, Steve Eder, Jan Ransom and Michael Rothfeld

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Moore para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El Centro Correccional Metropolitano en el Bajo Manhattan, al que Jeffrey Epstein llegó la tarde del 6 de julio de 2019 y donde murió 35 días después.

item.title: We Just Saw the Limit of American Military Might
item.description: How did the world’s richest nation, armed with the most powerful military, arrive at this strategic defeat?
item.dc:creator: W.J. Hennigan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ammar Awad/Reuters

item.title: America’s First Pope Pays Homage to Its First Saint
item.description: When Pope Leo visits Mother Cabrini’s birthplace, the message will not be their shared American nationality but a rebuke of American exceptionalism.
item.dc:creator: Kathleen Sprows Cummings

media:content.media:credit: Naila Ruechel for The New York Times

item.title: Georgia Vexes Trump Yet Again: 6 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primaries
item.description: Lt. Gov. Burt Jones lost the Republican runoff for governor to the health care executive Rick Jackson despite the president’s endorsement. Mr. Trump’s picks won in other races.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump’s chosen candidates won three Republican Senate primaries on Tuesday, but he but ran into trouble in other races in Georgia.

item.title: Los líderes de Europa se muestran amables con Trump tras una temporada tensa
item.description: Un acuerdo de paz con Irán y la esperanza de cooperar con Ucrania suavizaron el tono el martes durante una reunión del Grupo de los 7 celebrada en Francia.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Líderes mundiales, entre ellos el presidente Donald Trump, durante una sesión del Grupo de los 7 el martes en Évian-les-Bains, Francia

item.title: Quote of the Day: For 250th, a Maritime Pageant Daunting in Heft and Hassles
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, June 17, 2026

item.title: Ohio Governor Says State Should End the Death Penalty, Breaking With His Party
item.description: During a news conference on Tuesday, Gov. Mike DeWine, a Republican, called on state lawmakers to end all executions, a practice he once supported.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Gov. Mike DeWine’s denouncement of the policy on Tuesday puts him at odds with the president — and other party leaders in his state.

item.title: State Lawmaker and Trump-Backed Pastor Head to House Runoff in Oklahoma
item.description: The result sets up a Republican primary runoff between a right-wing firebrand and a more traditional candidate.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner and Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Michael Noble Jr. for The New York Times; Mark Tedford Campaign
media:content.media:description: Jackson Lahmeyer, left, a pastor, and State Representative Mark Tedford will face each other in an Aug. 25 runoff in Oklahoma’s First Congressional District, a solidly red, Tulsa-area seat.

item.title: Rep. Barry Moore Wins Senate Primary in Alabama, Holding Off Challenger
item.description: The contest to fill Alabama’s open Senate seat had become closer than expected between the House lawmaker and Jared Hudson, a political newcomer.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:credit: Michael Brochstein/Sipa USA, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Representative Barry Moore was endorsed by President Trump in his bid for the Senate.

item.title: Attorney General and Businessman Make Oklahoma Governor’s Runoff
item.description: Gentner Drummond, the attorney general, and Mike Mazzei, a newcomer who secured President Trump’s endorsement, emerged from a crowded field in the Republican primary.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:credit: Sue Ogrocki/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Attorney General Gentner Drummond of Oklahoma in Oklahoma City in 2023.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 17, 2026
item.description: Jonathan Raksin bets he can best us.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Cinthya Santos Briones for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Solving crosswords isn’t a competition, so call it a draw and read the column for help.

item.title: Trump Officials Challenge Reparations Program in Chicago Suburb
item.description: The Justice Department argued that the program, which seeks to compensate Black residents for housing discrimination, was racist and unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:credit: Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: “It is race discrimination, pure and simple. And it is illegal,” said Harmeet Dhillon, the assistant attorney general overseeing the Justice Department’s Civil Rights Division.

item.title: Rick Jackson, a Billionaire, Wins G.O.P. Runoff for Georgia Governor
item.description: Mr. Jackson, who had been largely unknown to voters just months ago, beat President Trump’s choice and will face Keisha Lance Bottoms, a Democrat, in November.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas

media:content.media:credit: Dustin Chambers for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rick Jackson speaks at a country club in Jefferson, GA.

item.title: Barn Fire Kills 17 Horses at Harness Track in Saratoga Springs, N.Y.
item.description: The cause is under investigation. Only one horse in the barn, Twin B Speed Dial, is believed to have survived, sustaining minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman

media:content.media:credit: Jim Franco/Albany Times Union, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: No people were injured in the fire, according to the local fire department. Aerial news footage showed the barn reduced mostly to ash by daylight.

item.title: As Mourning Begins, Deadly B-52 Crash Highlights Age of Bomber Fleet
item.description: Investigators are just beginning to determine what went wrong, but the Air Force has struggled to maintain the planes, which were first introduced in 1955.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler, Dave Philipps and John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The runway at Edwards Air Force Base in 2024.

item.title: Kevin Hern Wins Republican Primary for Oklahoma Senate Race
item.description: Mr. Hern, a congressman in the deep-red state, is now likely to be elected to the Senate to fill the seat of Markwayne Mullin, now the homeland security secretary.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Kevin Hern of Oklahoma had President Trump’s endorsement in his primary race for Senate.

item.title: U.S. Sues N.Y. State Over Troubled $11 Billion Home Health Care Program
item.description: The Justice Department accused officials of failing to police a vendor it said had siphoned off millions of dollars. A spokeswoman for Gov. Kathy Hochul called the suit an attempt to weaponize the justice system.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford and Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Michelle V. Agins/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dr. James V. McDonald, who leads the State Health Department, is a target of the Justice Department lawsuit.

item.title: Mike Collins, Endorsed by Trump, Wins Georgia Republican Senate Runoff
item.description: President Trump had made a late endorsement for Mr. Collins, who had campaigned as a MAGA candidate also able to appeal to moderates.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Audra Melton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Mike Collins has won the Republican primary runoff for Senate in Georgia. He will face Senator Jon Ossoff, a Democrat, in the fall.

item.title: Washington’s Reagan Airport Will Ground Flights for July 4 Events
item.description: The runways at Ronald Reagan National Airport will be closed after noon on July 4, and for several hours on July 3. Other celebrations surrounding the nation’s 250th birthday are also expected to cause disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian

media:content.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Flyovers, parachutists and fireworks over Washington will temporarily force Ronald Reagan National Airport to occasionally ground flights between late June and late August.

item.title: Europa dice estar lista para ayudar en el estrecho de Ormuz
item.description: Una coalición liderada por el Reino Unido y Francia se ha estado preparando para enviar dragaminas y otros barcos para garantizar la seguridad cuando se reanude el tráfico. El momento podría haber llegado.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Gregor Fischer/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: El dragaminas alemán Fulda podría ser desplegado en el estrecho de Ormuz.

item.title: Penny Brown Reynolds Wins Democratic Runoff for Georgia Secretary of State
item.description: Penny Brown Reynolds, a former judge, defeated Dana Barrett ahead of a potentially fraught general election to see who will succeed Brad Raffensperger as secretary of state.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen

media:content.media:credit: John Bazemore/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Penny Brown Reynolds, a former judge and senior civil rights official in the Biden administration.

item.title: Tim Fleming Wins GOP Runoff for Georgia Secretary of State
item.description: State Representative Tim Fleming defeated an ardent denier of the 2020 election results, Vernon Jones, in the Republican runoff to be Georgia’s top election official.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen
item.category: Georgia Criminal Case Against Trump (2020 Election)

media:content.media:credit: Brynn Anderson/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: State Representative Tim Fleming.

item.title: What Polls Say About Alabama’s Senate Primary Runoff
item.description: Representative Barry Moore beat Jared Hudson by 14 percentage points in the Republican primary, but runoff polls offer a wide range of potential outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Evan Reynolds

media:content.media:credit: Mike Stewart/Associated Press, Michael Brochstein/Sipa USA, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Jared Hudson, a former Navy SEAL, led in two recent runoff polls, and Representative Barry Moore led in one.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Jared Hudson, Republican Senate Candidate in Alabama
item.description: He is a former sniper with the Navy SEALs who is trying to spring an upset over President Trump’s preferred candidate.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Mike Stewart/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Jared Hudson, a Republican candidate for Senate in Alabama, has put faith at the center of his campaign.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Barry Moore, Republican Senate Candidate in Alabama
item.description: He was an early supporter of President Trump’s first campaign. Now, he has Mr. Trump’s backing.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Michael Brochstein/Sipa, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Representative Barry Moore, Republican of Alabama, has received support from the crypto industry.

item.title: The Crypto Industry Makes Its Biggest Midterm Bet Yet
item.description: The industry’s main super PAC has spent $9.8 million on ads to help the Senate bid of Representative Barry Moore of Alabama, a Republican seen as pro-cryptocurrency.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Representative Barry Moore has voted for several bills that the crypto industry has prioritized this Congress.

item.title: Obama’s Nuclear Deal Looms Over Trump’s Iran Negotiations
item.description: President Trump is under pressure to significantly improve upon the Obama-era deal in order to justify the huge human and economic cost of taking the United States to war.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump campaigned against the Obama-era deal as far back as 2015, and ultimately killed it during his first term over the objections of many of his top national security aides.

item.title: Internal Emails Raise Questions About D.H.S. Ending Haiti Protections
item.description: Newly released documents could complicate deliberations by the Supreme Court over whether the Trump administration can immediately cancel deportation protections for some migrants.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Ann E. Marimow

media:content.media:credit: Roberto Schmidt/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Guerline Jozef, co-founder and executive director of Haitian Bridge Alliance, in March. A Supreme Court decision on the Trump administration’s handling of humanitarian protections for Haitians is anticipated by the end of the month.

item.title: Areas to Watch in Georgia’s Republican Primary Runoffs
item.description: Georgia’s Republican runoffs are expected to follow urban-rural divides, with an open question of how recent endorsements will shift support.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Polls close in Georgia at 7 p.m. Eastern.

item.title: Beijing’s New Message to Its Citizens: Your Money Belongs at Home
item.description: Eager to keep capital within its borders, China is restricting the ways individuals can engage with global markets.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan

media:content.media:credit: Dongyan Xu

item.title: Good Luck, Kevin Warsh. You’ll Need It.
item.description: Between a surge in interest rates, increasingly restive colleagues and the demands of President Trump, the new Fed chairman, Kevin Warsh, is in a bind.
item.dc:creator: Steven Rattner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Alex Brandon/Associated Press

item.title: Red Curry Chicken and Rice Bowls
item.description: These cozy yet reviving rice bowls pair Thai red curry-stained rice with tender chicken and a citrusy slaw.

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Vanni for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Spencer Richards.

item.title: Julia Child’s Berry Clafoutis
item.description: This recipe is for a delicately sweet dessert whose elegance should not distract from its ease (it can be made while the rest of dinner is in the oven). Make sure you have fresh berries, and serve the result warm.

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Vanni for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Spencer Richards.
